primary_accounting_issue: Appréciation du critère SPPI en IFRS 9 pour un actif financier dont des clauses contractuelles peuvent modifier le calendrier ou le montant des flux de trésorerie en cas d’événement déclencheur.

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the strict precedence rule that an IFRS Standard governs over any IAS material on the same issue; here IFRS 9 directly governs classification of financial assets and the SPPI assessment, while no higher-precedence overlapping document displaces it.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: low — the question is directly about the IFRS 9 contractual cash flow characteristics test for financial assets, and the cited paragraphs explicitly address contingent terms that change timing or amount of cash flows.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern whether contractual cash flows with contingent or modifying terms can still satisfy SPPI and how that affects classification.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs clarify the meaning of a basic lending arrangement, illustrate qualifying and non-qualifying terms, and constrain the SPPI analysis.

peripheral_authority.reason: These paragraphs concern separation of components in insurance contracts and do not help identify peer classification approaches for SPPI under IFRS 9.
These paragraphs address liability-versus-equity classification for issuers, not the SPPI-based classification of financial assets.
These paragraphs relate to hedge accounting and hedged items, which are topically related to financial instruments but not relevant to identifying peer approaches for the SPPI issue.

treatment_families.family: Financial assets meeting the SPPI condition and held to collect contractual cash flows
Financial assets meeting the SPPI condition and held both to collect contractual cash flows and to sell
Financial assets failing the SPPI condition or otherwise not qualifying for the SPPI-based categories

approaches.label: Amortised cost
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies amortised cost as a named classification model when the business model is hold to collect and the contractual cash flows satisfy SPPI.

approaches.label: Fair value through other comprehensive income
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value through other comprehensive income as a named classification model when the business model is collect and sell and the contractual cash flows satisfy SPPI.

approaches.label: Fair value through profit or loss
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value through profit or loss as the remaining classification model when the asset does not qualify for the SPPI-based categories.
